fields.icd_conflict_detail: All three documents (referral form, clinical notes, and prescription) consistently list ICD-10 code M19.011 with the description 'Primary osteoarthritis, right shoulder.' No conflict was detected across documents.
fields.priority_reason: Patient is post-surgical for a chronic musculoskeletal condition (right shoulder osteoarthritis) with documented difficulty in activities of daily living, warranting medium priority as the case involves post-operative recovery but not an acute fracture or neurological emergency.
fields.notes: 1. The medical justification references 'safe ambulation and fall prevention,' which is more consistent with a mobility aid (e.g., walker or cane) than a hospital bed — this clinical mismatch may warrant clarification with Dr. Chen. 2. Physician NPI is missing across all three documents and should be obtained before billing. 3. Authorization Reference Number is blank — adjuster Sarah Mitchell should be contacted to obtain prior authorization before equipment is dispatched. 4. Patient height is 6'4" / 157 lbs, which appears underweight for that height (BMI ~19.1) — may be worth flagging for clinical review. 5. Referral was submitted 12 days after the injury date (08/15/2026 injury, 08/27/2026 submission), and no auth has been secured yet.

email_body:
Dear Ms. Mitchell,

I hope this message finds you well. I am reaching out regarding a durable medical equipment (DME) referral for your insured, **Louis Barnes**, under workers' compensation claim **WC-2026-084501**.

While processing this referral, we have identified the following required items that are currently missing from the file:

1. **Authorization Reference Number (auth_ref)** *(Hard Block — routing is on hold pending receipt)*
2. **Prescribing Physician NPI (physician_npi)**

Please note that the missing authorization reference number is preventing us from advancing this referral further. As a result, processing is currently on hold until this information is received.

To avoid any delay in care for Mr. Barnes, we kindly ask that you provide the outstanding information **within 24 business hours** of receiving this notice. You may reply directly to this email or contact our intake team at your earliest convenience.

Should you have any questions or require clarification, please do not hesitate to reach out.

Thank you for your prompt attention to this matter.

Warm regards,
**Coastal DME Intake Agent**
Coastal DME | Intake Department
